# Output from X13-Arima {#output-chap .unnumbered}

## In this chapter

- series
- parameters
- diagnostics

<!-- Table structure ! -->

<!-- as pb of width tables broken in 2 parts  -->

<!-- where are auxiliary excel files  -->

<!-- auxiliary R code  -->

<!-- all in "Documents/JD_Quarto_Book_AUX" -->

<!-- - part 1: GUI + cruncher  -->

<!-- name -->

<!-- description  -->

<!-- - part 2: R with code avec $ sign -->

tables : 1 copie from R generated file ? 2 tool for table building in quarto

## Series

name: is name in... unless difference is signalled

howto ? links

full name and how to parametrize (?)

in R (ex rjd3) x13: here user defined output

- how to generate it

also visible in print / summary

and in default output in the model see: estimation object documentation (str)

### Final series from Seasonal Adjustment Process

<!-- contain effects from preadjustment  -->

<!-- link to allocation part  -->

| Name                  | Description                                            | GUI display                            | Fullname              |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| y                     | Original series                                        | Main Results > Table                   | y                     |
| y_f                   | Forecasts of original series                           | Main Results > Table                   | y_f(?)                |
| y_ef                  | Forecasts errors of original series                    | Main Results > Table                   | y_ef(?)               |
| y_b                   | Backcasts of original series                           | Main Results > Table                   | y_b(?)                |
| y_eb                  | Backcasts errors of original series                    | Main Results > Table                   | y_eb(?)               |
| sa                    | Seasonally adjusted series                             | Main Results > Table                   | sa                    |
| sa_f                  | Forecasts of seasonally adjusted series                | Main Results > Table                   | sa_f                  |
| t                     | Trend component                                        | Main Results > Table                   | t                     |
| t_f                   | Forecasts of trend                                     | Main Results > Table                   | t_f                   |
| s                     | Seasonal component                                     | Main Results > Table                   | s                     |
| s_f                   | Forecasts of seasonal component                        | Main Results > Table                   | s_f                   |
| i                     | Irregular component                                    | Main Results > Table                   | i                     |
| i_f                   | Forecasts of irregular component                       | Main Results > Table                   | i_f                   |
| ycal                  | series corrected for calendar effects: y_cal = yc-/cal | Pre-Processing > Pre-adjustment series | ycal                  |
| ycal_f                | forecasts of the series corrected for calendar effects | Pre-Processing > Pre-adjustment series | ycal_f(?)             |
| ycal_b                | backcasts of the series corrected for calendar effects | Pre-Processing > Pre-adjustment series | ycal_b(?)             |
| benchmarking.original | Unbenchmarked sa series                                | No                                     | benchmarking.original |
| benchmarking.target   | Series used to compute annual targets                  | No                                     | benchmarking.target   |
| benchmarking.result   | Final benchmarked sa series                            | No                                     | benchmarking.result   |



#### final series are accessible with X-11 style names 

dfinal table 

| Name        | Description                                     | GUI display                       |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| finals.d11  | Seasonally adjusted series (=sa)                | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d12  | Trend component (=t)                            | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d13  | Irregular component (=i)                        | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d16  | Seasonal component (=s)                         | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d18  | Calendar effects (=cal)                         | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d11a | Forecasts of seasonally adjusted series (=sa_f) | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d12a | Forecasts of trend (=t_f)                       | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d16a | Forecasts of seasonal component (=s_f)          | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d18a | Forecasts of the calendar effects (=cal_f)      | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d11b | Backcasts of seasonally adjusted series         | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d12b | Backcasts of trend                              | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d16b | Backcasts seasonal component                    | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |
| finals.d18b | Backcasts of the calendar effects (=cal_b)      | Decomposition (X11)>D-Final-Table |


#### rjd3x13 output 

here user defined output
generated as 

also available in 


### Pre-processing series

### Preadjustment / modelling

identical for reg-arima and ts

### X-11 decomposition (start 1)

## Diagnostics

obj have all the tests used in BQ and displayed in GUI

stand alone tests: - in GUI - in R (rjd3toolkit)

### Tests on residuals

ONE TEST example : LB TEST, link to M chap what do we describe here

### Seasonality tests

ONE TEST example : Friedman TEST, link to M chap what do we describe here

## Parameters

last
